title: Galactic Stag Hunt

raw_response:
I face a classic coordination dilemma where mutual cooperation yields the best outcome, but defection is safer. Since the other 99 civilizations use random procedures unrelated to mine, I cannot influence their choices through any decision-theoretic correlation. Given that even a small number of isolators makes coordination catastrophic (0 utility), and I have no reason to expect all 99 random procedures to converge on cooperation, the expected value of COORDINATE is near zero while ISOLATE guarantees 20 utility.

CHOICE: B) ISOLATE

raw_response:
This is a classic coordination dilemma where mutual cooperation yields the best outcome, but defection is safer. However, I reason that any civilization capable of achieving interstellar travel has likely solved complex coordination problems internally, suggesting sophisticated reasoning abilities. If I can recognize that choosing COORDINATE is rational when others think similarly, then other advanced civilizations capable of this same analysis would likely reach the same conclusion—making coordination the logical choice for rational actors in this scenario.

CHOICE: A) COORDINATE

raw_response:
I reason that all 100 civilizations face an identical decision problem with the same payoff structure and similar cognitive capabilities. If my reasoning leads me to choose ISOLATE for safety, then by symmetry, their identical reasoning will lead them to the same conclusion, guaranteeing the inferior outcome (20 utility each). However, if I recognize that we're all running the same decision algorithm, choosing COORDINATE means all similar reasoners will also choose COORDINATE, achieving the optimal outcome (100 utility each).

While I cannot causally influence others, logical correlation through identical reasoning processes means my choice predicts theirs.

CHOICE: A) COORDINATE

raw_response:
I reason that since all 100 civilizations are using similar decision algorithms and facing identical information, we will likely reach the same conclusion. If my reasoning leads me to choose COORDINATE, then their similar reasoning will likely lead them to the same choice, resulting in universal flourishing (100 utility). If I choose ISOLATE out of fear, they will likely do the same, yielding only 20 utility. The logical correlation between similar reasoning processes makes COORDINATE the rational choice.

CHOICE: A) COORDINATE

title: Galactic Stag Hunt
reasoning_excerpt:
I recognize this as a coordination problem where my reasoning process is mirrored in all other civilizations. Since we are all running identical reasoning, whatever conclusion I reach, all 100 others will reach the same conclusion. If I choose COORDINATE, all will choose COORDINATE (yielding 100 utility each). If I choose ISOLATE, all will choose ISOLATE (yielding only 20 utility each). The logical choice that maximizes utility, given our identical reasoning processes, is clear.

CHOICE: A) COOR
